2606.24428.title: Escaping the Self-Confirmation Trap: An Execute-Distill-Verify Paradigm for Agentic Experience Learning
2606.24428.paper_summary: Experience-driven self-evolution is critical for large language model (LLM) agents to improve through open-world interaction. However, existing experience learning methods mostly rely on single-agent loops, where the same agent executes tasks, summarizes outcomes, and determines memory content. This setup makes agents vulnerable to the Self-Confirmation Trap: wrong-but-self-consistent trajectories are misidentified as successful experience, leading to cumulative errors during retrieval and reuse. To address this issue, we propose EDV, an Execute-Distill-Verify framework for reliable experience learning. In the Execute stage, multiple heterogeneous agents explore the same task space in parallel to generate diverse candidate trajectories. In the Distill stage, a dedicated third-party agent comparatively analyzes these trajectories to produce candidate experiences, reducing executor-centric summarization bias. In the Verify stage, the execution group validates candidates via a consensus mechanism, and only approved experiences are written into shared or private memory. By decoupling the three stages, EDV transforms experience learning from isolated self-reflection into collaborative construction, filtering erroneous and noisy content before memory insertion. We evaluate EDV on three challenging long-horizon benchmarks: tau2-bench, Mind2Web and MMTB. Results show EDV consistently outperforms strong baselines, validating that reliable experience construction is essential for robust agent self-evolution. Our code is available at https://github.com/shidingz/EDV.
2606.24428.paper_summary_zh: 經驗驅動的自我演化對於大型語言模型（LLM）代理在開放世界互動中進行改進至關重要。<lb/>然而，現有的經驗學習方法大多依賴於單一代理循環，其中同一代理執行任務、總結結果並確定記憶內容。<lb/>這種設置使代理容易受到自我確認陷阱的影響：錯誤但自洽的軌跡被錯誤地識別為成功的經驗，導致在檢索和重用過程中出現累積錯誤。<lb/>為了解決這個問題，我們提出了EDV，一個可靠的經驗學習框架，名為執行-提煉-驗證。<lb/>在執行階段，多個異質代理並行探索相同的任務空間，以生成多樣的候選軌跡。<lb/>在提煉階段，一個專門的第三方代理比較分析這些軌跡，以產生候選經驗，減少以執行者為中心的總結偏見。<lb/>在驗證階段，執行小組通過共識機制驗證候選者，只有經過批准的經驗才會寫入共享或私有記憶。<lb/>通過解耦這三個階段，EDV將經驗學習從孤立的自我反思轉變為協作建構，在記憶插入之前過濾錯誤和噪音內容。<lb/>我們在三個具有挑戰性的長期基準上評估EDV：tau2-bench、Mind2Web和MMTB。<lb/>結果顯示，EDV始終優於強基準，驗證了可靠的經驗構建對於穩健的代理自我演化至關重要。<lb/>我們的代碼可在 https://github.com/shidingz/EDV 獲得。
2606.24428.author: Shiding Zhu et.al.
2606.24428.authors: Shiding Zhu, Yudi Qi, Yajie Wang, Jiaze Li, Chao Song, Yaorui Shi, Yibo Miao, Hanqi Gao, Kai Zhang
